generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed commands from ACC algorithms into motor voltage commands for the vehicle's propulsion system via Vedder Electronic Speed Controller"
]

instance SteeringServoController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Handles steering commands from ACC algorithms, interfacing with the vehicle's steering servo mechanism"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "2D LiDAR sensor (Hokuyo UST-10LX) providing raw point cloud data for object detection and environmental mapping"
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera capturing spatial data for object detection and environmental perception"
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance RawLiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR
    base:desc "Raw 2D LiDAR point cloud data (plane coordinates) processed by Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking software"
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR
    base:desc "Processed object tracks and environmental maps generated from raw sensor data via clustering and tracking algorithms"
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera
    base:desc "Vehicle state metrics including motor speed, steering position, and general vehicle metrics transmitted via ROS2 topics"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LeadVehicleTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model implementing lead vehicle detection and tracking algorithms for ACC functionality"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance SpeedRegulationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model containing speed regulation algorithms based on distance to preceding vehicles"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LeadVehicleTrackingModel, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalPerceptionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric and behavioral model representing the vehicle's operational environment for ACC decision making"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance RawLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw 2D LiDAR point cloud data (plane coordinates) before processing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointCloud
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedObjectData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object detection data (object tracks, trajectories) after clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedObjectTracks
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LeadVehicleTrackingModel, EnvironmentalPerceptionModel
]

instance NormalizedVehicleState : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Normalized vehicle state data (speed, position, steering) aligned with digital twin models"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SpeedRegulationAlgorithm, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance SpeedCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Desired speed command output from ACC algorithms for vehicle actuation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SpeedRegulationAlgorithm
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleSpeedController
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component performing clustering and tracking of raw LiDAR point cloud data to generate object tracks and environmental maps"
    DTDFVocab:enables LeadVehicleTrackingModel, EnvironmentalPerceptionModel
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity implementing data translation, unit conversions (e.g., m/s to km/h), and message standardization across heterogeneous data streams"
    DTDFVocab:enables LeadVehicleTrackingModel, SpeedRegulationAlgorithm, NormalizedVehicleState
]

instance ROS2CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware framework (ROS2) enabling real-time communication between physical and digital components via publish-subscribe model"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleSpeedController, SteeringServoController
]

instance WiFiUDPTransport : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Transport layer mechanism using Wi-Fi UDP for cross-vehicle data exchange between physical and digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleStateData, SpeedCommandData
]

instance SQLDatabaseStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structured data storage system (SQL database) for saving vehicle state logs, performance reports, and analytics"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization service displaying vehicle state data (speed, position, sensor readings) and lead vehicle tracking via user interface"
    DTDFVocab:provides LiveVehicleDashboard, ObjectTrackingVisualization
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC-specific computations (lead vehicle detection, speed calculation) from onboard ECUs to cloud-based infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:provides SpeedCommandGeneration, AlgorithmValidation
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operator-facing remote control interface enabling ACC mode management, custom speed settings, and emergency braking commands"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCModeControl, EmergencyBrakingTrigger, DeveloperDiagnostics
]

instance DataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated data logging system saving vehicle state data, performance metrics, and analytics for offline analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides StateDataLogging, PerformanceReportGeneration
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance LiveVehicleDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization interface showing current vehicle speed, position, sensor readings, and lead vehicle tracking data"
]

instance ObjectTrackingVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic visualization of detected objects (vehicles, pedestrians) with projected driving paths and object trajectories"
]

instance SpeedCommandGeneration : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation of desired speed commands based on distance to lead vehicle and ACC algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AlgorithmValidation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Validation of ACC algorithms through cloud-based computation offloading and performance benchmarking"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCModeControl : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote enable/disable control of Adaptive Cruise Control system mode"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingTrigger : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote triggering of emergency braking commands with 100ms latency for safety-critical operations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DeveloperDiagnostics : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Developer-facing troubleshooting interface with real-time diagnostics, data collection, and performance benchmarking"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance StateDataLogging : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated logging of vehicle state data (speed, position, sensor readings) for post-processing analytics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceReportGeneration : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation of performance reports and analytics for ACC system evaluation and optimization"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance VehicleOperationalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera, VehicleSpeedController, SteeringServoController
]

instance DigitalTwinInfrastructure : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LeadVehicleTrackingModel, SpeedRegulationAlgorithm, EnvironmentalPerceptionModel,
        DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2CommunicationEnabler, WiFiUDPTransport, SQLDatabaseStorage,
        RealTimeMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance HumanOperatorInterface : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiveVehicleDashboard, ObjectTrackingVisualization, ACCModeControl,
        EmergencyBrakingTrigger, DeveloperDiagnostics, StateDataLogging, PerformanceReportGeneration
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Not in Document**
  *(No explicit details provided about control flows from digital to physical, including command types, validation, or feedback systems.)*
 "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system enforces **strict time synchronization** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and real-time coordination. The system mandates **clock synchronization accuracy of ≤100 ms** for safety-critical vehicular applications, driven by hardware limitations and the need for precise temporal alignment across distributed entities. This requirement ensures deterministic timing for critical operations such as sensor data processing, actuation commands, and data pre-processing, where even minor delays could compromise system safety or performance. The temporal granularity is aligned with the operational cadence of automotive systems, where millisecond-level precision is often essential for tasks like point cloud clustering and tracking within the Physical Twin Entity.
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system follows an **entity-centric architecture** rooted in ISO 23247 RA, with three core entities mapped to hardware components:
    - **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts compute, sensors, actuators, and general automotive functions (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) via ROS2 packages.
    - **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data pre-processing, actuation, and control mechanisms, diverging from ISO 23247 by encapsulating broader system resources.
    - **User Entity**: Contains the singular _User Interface FE_, split into **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** components, executed as ROS2 nodes.
    The orchestration relies on **ROS2’s modularity**, where each FE is implemented as an independent package, enabling dynamic integration and scalability. **Component integration** is facilitated via ROS2’s topic/service model, ensuring inter-entity communication. Resource management is implicit in the entity-based deployment, where hardware ownership aligns with functional ownership. The system’s **system-wide coordination** is implicit in the ISO 23247 RA alignment, though deviations (e.g., expanded Physical Twin Entity scope) introduce customization trade-offs.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The system emphasizes **high-fidelity digital representation** of OMEs, ensuring data integrity via **clock synchronization** for real-time data exchange. While the ISO 23247 framework mandates **data integrity**, validation and verification methods, uncertainty quantification, and error correction mechanisms are **not explicitly detailed** in the documents. The Digital Twin Entity’s fidelity is implicitly tied to the accuracy of the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) and its digital twin alignment, but no specific methodologies (e.g., ISO/IEC 15288) or quality assurance processes are referenced.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **Intra-entity (within Digital Twin):** ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for software component communication.
    - **Inter-entity (physical ↔ digital):** Wi-Fi over **UDP** (User Datagram Protocol) in the transport layer.

  - **Network Topology & Architecture:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Localized, likely on the vehicle’s onboard system (edge computing).
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** Connects physical and digital twins over a **broadband Wi-Fi network** (e.g., cloud/edge gateway).

  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements:**
    - **Real-time criticality:** LiDAR data (point clouds) and vehicle state updates require **low latency** (likely <100ms for steering/motor commands).
    - **Bandwidth:** Not specified, but high-throughput required for LiDAR (e.g., 100+ Mbps for point cloud streams).

  - **Reliability and Redundancy:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Built-in fault tolerance (e.g., message retries, deadlock handling).
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** No explicit redundancy, but UDP’s packet loss handling may mitigate interruptions.
    - **No mention of hardware redundancy** (e.g., dual LiDAR sensors) or failover mechanisms.

  - **Security Protocols:**
    - **Data Translation FE:** Ensures **unit conversions and message alignment**, but **no explicit security** (e.g., encryption, authentication) is described.
    - **ROS2 Network:** May use ROS security plugins (e.g., TLS for message encryption), but not detailed.
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** Vulnerable to eavesdropping; **no mention of encryption** (e.g., WPA3, TLS) for inter-entity communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure:**
  - **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Edge Computing:** Primary deployment (e.g., onboard vehicle systems for real-time processing).
    - **Cloud/On-Premise:** Likely used for **data storage, reporting, and visualization** (e.g., 3D cockpit).
    - **No explicit mention of hybrid cloud** or distributed architectures.

  - **Computational and Storage Requirements:**
    - **Real-time Processing:**
      - **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking:** Requires **high-performance CPUs/GPUs** (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson for LiDAR processing).
      - **ROS2 Nodes:** Lightweight but scalable for multiple sensors (e.g., motor/steering actuation).
    - **Storage:**
      - **Data Storage FE:** Handles raw/processed data (e.g., LiDAR logs, vehicle telemetry).
      - **Reporting FE:** Generates summaries (e.g., driving paths, diagnostics) for long-term storage.

  - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Supports **horizontal scaling** (e.g., adding more nodes for sensor fusion).
    - **Edge-Cloud Hybrid:** Offloads heavy computations (e.g., 3D visualization) to cloud if needed.
    - **No explicit mention of auto-scaling** (e.g., Kubernetes) or dynamic resource allocation.

  - **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - **Onboard Vehicle (Edge):** Primary for real-time control (e.g., motor/steering actuation).
    - **Cloud-Based:** For **reporting, visualization, and historical data analysis**.
    - **No mention of IoT gateways** or microservices architectures.

  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Built-in resilience (e.g., message retries, deadlock prevention).
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** No redundancy; **no SLA guarantees** for inter-entity communication.
    - **No explicit redundancy** (e.g., dual power supplies, failover switches) is described.
    - **Availability:** Implied to be **high** for onboard systems but **not quantified** (e.g., 99.9% uptime).
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
    Not in Document
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard (Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing) is referenced, specifically Part 2: Reference Architecture, which defines four core entities:
  1. **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Represents physical assets (e.g., equipment, facilities, processes) with observable attributes.
  2. **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data acquisition from OMEs and controls their operations.
  3. **Digital Twin Entity**: Provides a digital replica of the OME, including its state, behavior, and historical data.
  4. **User Entity**: Facilitates human interaction via interfaces (e.g., monitoring dashboards, control panels).

  The standard does not mandate specific technologies but suggests **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** as an example protocol for Functional Entities (FEs) to enable interoperability between entities. No additional standards (e.g., certification frameworks, compliance frameworks) or interoperability specifications are mentioned in the provided documents."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system must adhere to the performance and operational requirements of **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** as specified in **ISO 15622:2018**, ensuring compliance with its test procedures for system accuracy, latency, and reliability. For **Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication**, the system must implement the **3GPP TS 22.185: Release 15** service requirements, which define the technical framework for **Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V)**, **Vehicle-to-Infrastructure (V2I)**, and **Vehicle-to-Network (V2N)** interactions, including secure data exchange frameworks.

The system prioritizes **safety-critical operations**, aligning with the functional safety standards of the automotive domain. While no explicit mention of **ISO 26262-1:2018** (Functional Safety for Road Vehicles) is present, the underlying architecture implies adherence to **ASIL (Automotive Safety Integrity Level) requirements** for critical control functions, ensuring fail-safe mechanisms and risk mitigation for autonomous driving scenarios. Access control mechanisms are implied through the structured communication protocols of V2X services, though no specific authentication systems (e.g., digital signatures, PKI) or threat mitigation strategies (e.g., intrusion detection) are detailed in the documents."
]
